---
title: "Lab 2 - Data wrangling"
author: "Luke Owen"
date: "March 16, 2026"
format:  
  html: default # for speed
  pdf: default # for final output

---


```{r}
#| eval: true
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
```


# Questions

## Part 1

### Question 1

```{r}
midwest |>
  count(state, name = "n_counties") |>
  arrange(desc(n_counties))
```
Illinois has the highest number of counties, with 102. Wisconsin has the lowest number of counties, with 72.
\newpage

### Question 2

```{r}
midwest |>
  count(county, name = "n") |>
  filter(n == 5)
```

There are three counties that share names across all states. Crawford, Jackson, and Monroe.
\newpage

### Question 3


\newpage

### Question 4
\newpage

### Question 5

\newpage

### Question 6

\newpage

### Question 7

\newpage

### Question 8

\newpage

## Part 2


### Question 9
